title: droguetia iners subsp. iners
intro: Droguetia iners subsp. iners, also known as the "Iners Droguetia," is a species of flowering plant in the family Achariaceae. It is a woody climber native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia, known for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The Iners Droguetia is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its medicinal compounds.
